text: When designing a REST API, following best practices ensures your system is user-friendly and maintainable. Your API's endpoints should be clear and logical; for example, using `/users` rather than `/getAllUsers`. Appropriate status codes are essential—use `200` for successful requests or `404` if a resource is not found. Thorough documentation is a must; it explains how developers can interact with your API. Finally, always consider versioning. By including a version in the URL or header, like `/v2/users`, you allow for updates without breaking existing integrations. These habits help developers build reliable and scalable applications on your platform.
